Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents With Proof Of Service
Off Calendar May-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PETER SUTER's Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Peter Suter's motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in his Requests for Admission, Set One, to plaintiff Eric McFadden is granted. McFadden failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set One, is deemed admitted by McFadden. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).)

Suter does not seek sanctions. However, CCP 2033.280(c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $50 payable by McFadden to Suter. McFadden shall remit sanctions within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Motion To Compel Plaintiff Eric Mcfaddens Responses Without Objections To Form And Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production, Set One With Proof Of Service
Off Calendar May-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PETER SUTER's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Eric Mcfaddens Responses Without Objections To Form And Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production, Set One.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Peter Suter's motion to compel Plaintiff Eric McFadden to respond to his Form and Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, is granted. McFadden failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2030.290(a), 2031.300(a).) McFadden is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, accompanied by responsive documents within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Suter does not seek sanctions. The court awards none.

The court notes intervenor Cody Molica's notice of his incarceration filed on April 30, 2025. Because this motion seeks no relief against Molica, no accommodation is provided.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
